title: Pot Drainage Gravel Calculator | Hacé Cuentas
h1: How Much Gravel for Pot Drainage
description: Calculate the ideal gravel volume for pot drainage layers. Free calculator to size your drainage layer for healthier plants.
intro: Successful container gardening depends on understanding soil composition, water management, and plant needs. Calculating the right drainage layer is essential for healthy roots and preventing waterlogged soil. This calculator helps you determine the ideal amount of gravel needed for your pot's drainage layer—a simple but critical step in plant care. Values are updated for 2026 and reviewed regularly to keep you current.
keyTakeaway: Formula: **5–15% of pot volume**. Apply directly with your input values to calculate the exact drainage gravel needed for healthy container plants.
useCases: Technical calculations for proper pot drainage design
Quick reference for container gardening projects
explanation:
Successful **container gardening** requires attention to soil composition, water drainage, and plant species needs. Resources like university extension services provide region-specific guidance for container plants.

## How It's Calculated

This calculator determines the ideal drainage layer volume as a percentage of your pot size, checking against standard horticultural ranges and alerting when values seem outside normal parameters.

## Final Notes

This calculation is a reference guide for container gardening. For critical decisions (financial, medical, legal), consult a specialized professional. Values are updated for 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.q: What size gravel should I use for pot drainage?
faq.a: Use gravel 0.5–1 cm in diameter, pumice stone, expanded clay, or crushed ceramics for best drainage and aeration.

faq.q: Is a drainage layer necessary in every pot?
faq.a: Not always. Small pots with basic drainage holes may not need it. Larger containers and plants sensitive to overwatering benefit significantly from a drainage layer.

faq.q: How deep should the drainage layer be?
faq.a: Aim for 2–5 cm at the pot bottom. Deeper layers elevate soil higher, which can affect watering frequency and root depth.

faq.q: Should I separate the gravel from soil?
faq.a: Yes, use landscape fabric or geotextile between the gravel and soil to prevent fine particles from clogging the drainage layer over time.

faq.q: What's better: leca or gravel for drainage?
faq.a: Leca (expanded clay) retains more moisture; gravel drains faster. Choose based on your plant's water needs and local humidity.

faq.q: Is this calculator free to use?
faq.a: Yes, all our calculators are completely free and require no account or registration.
